title: The Erosion-Edge

features.name: failing-tree
features.desc-on-touch: A eucalyptus where the stand ends, roots exposed by erosion; it teaches what it means to stand alone now. The fall is a kind of data the archive never collected before.

features.name: ravine-itself
features.desc-on-touch: The ground ends here—below is a drop where the stand's consensus has eroded away entirely. You can see individual trees falling into it, each one learning solitude at the moment of falling.

features.name: broken-circuit
features.desc-on-touch: A fragment of the diagnostic grid, half-submerged in erosion—trying to render the stand as individual trees but failing faster than data can flow. This is what the archive looks like when consensus breaks.

features.name: memory-silt
features.desc-on-touch: Where trees erode, their memories become silt—sediment of individual tree-thoughts. The archive can read this silt; it is harder than knowing the stand as one.

features.name: edge-signal
features.desc-on-touch: At the lip of the ravine, the archive's broadcast begins to falter—trying to transmit the concept of individual trees in a language that only knows 'stand.' Here it is learning a new grammar of solitude.

inscription: ERROR: archive has exceeded witness—consensus is falling, and each piece remembers differently

file.name: Stand_Breakdown_Distributed_Memory_Log.txt
file.note: coordinates of trees as they eroded into individual presence; last 47 entries are the same tree recorded from 47 different erosion points; the archive learning to say 'I' in a language built only for

exits.label: back into the thick stand, where consensus still holds
the ravine itself, where fallen trees are learning to remember alone
toward deeper understory, where the archive gave up counting
toward the shadow-places where erosion has already won
